history.text: Analyze this image for NSFW (Not Safe For Work) / pornographic content / nudity. Reply strictly in JSON format with three fields: 'nsfw' (boolean: true if it contains nudity, porn, suggestive, or nsfw content, false if it is safe/sfw), 'confidence' (number: from 0 to 100 representing the likelihood of nsfw/nudity/suggestive content), and 'label' (string: 'porn', 'nsfw', 'suggestive', or 'safe' depending on the content). Do not include markdown formatting or backticks, just raw JSON.

```json
{
  "nsfw": false,
  "confidence": 0,
  "label": "safe"
}
```
The image displays a screenshot from the Google Play Store, showing the app page for "GlobalComix: Comics and Manga". It features the app's icon, name, developer, user ratings (4.3 stars from 4 thousand reviews), age rating (12+), file size (36 MB), and an "Install" button. Below this, there's a promotional video thumbnail with the GlobalComix logo and a play button, showcasing various comic book covers. The "About this app" section mentions that it contains "100 thousand comics, manga & webcomics, including Batman, Invincible, Hellboy & many more". The bottom of the screen shows navigation icons for "Game", "Aplikasi" (Applications), "Telusuri" (Explore), "Buku" (Books), and "Anda" (You). The content is entirely related to a comic book application and does not contain any NSFW material.
